customer_anxiety_profile: Andrew communicates in short, fragmented sentences throughout — likely a language barrier rather than hostility. He asked for price information three times and preferred email over a phone call. His compliance at close was passive ('I'll wait for your call tomorrow') rather than enthusiastic. He may not feel committed to the appointment.
unresolved_concerns: Andrew asked for price/cost information three times and received only a deferral — he will likely open the closer call by asking for price immediately
Andrew's channel preference was email, not phone — the closer should acknowledge this and move quickly to value to justify the call format
Co-owner/spouse status was never confirmed — unknown whether anyone else is on the title or involved in the decision
sensitive_topics_raised: Language barrier — Andrew's responses were fragmented and he explicitly said 'please repeat, I don't understand' at 01:52; the closer should speak slowly and confirm comprehension
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for tomorrow at 10AM by phone
Rep said 'I'll start on your design now' — Andrew expects a design to be ready
Rep said 'I'll make sure we show you that right away so we won't waste your time' — Andrew expects price to be shown at the start of the call
closer_should_know: Andrew asked for price three times and was told to wait for the call — open with the design and price in the first two minutes or he will disengage. Also: the setter may have called Andrew 'Audrey' at close (transcript shows this at 05:01); use 'Andrew' and do not reference the setter call in a way that surfaces the name error.
